surface.name: Tailscale browser-led quickstart
surface.selection_basis: Tailscale's install documentation directly routes new users to the Tailscale quickstart, which explicitly creates a tailnet, installs the client on two devices, displays both devices, and redirects to the admin console.

alternatives_considered.name: Install client first
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The quickstart permits this order, but the browser-led Get Started path includes account and plan setup in one sequence.

alternatives_considered.name: Server auth-key onboarding
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a later device-addition method; the primary quickstart uses interactive SSO on personal machines.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a private tailnet and enroll two devices into it.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived at the Tailscale quickstart.
No tailnet or enrolled devices exist yet.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A new tailnet contains two authenticated devices, both are visible in onboarding, and the developer reaches the Tailscale admin console.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: After the second device authenticates, both devices display; selecting Take me home opens the admin console.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official Create a tailnet sequence ends after both devices display and the user is redirected to the admin console.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: It completes the named private-network creation sequence before optional renaming, DNS, invites, routing, and policies.

prerequisites.requirement: A supported SSO identity-provider account is required for signup and device authentication.
Two supported devices are needed to complete the documented two-device tailnet sequence.

primary_path.action: Go to tailscale.com, select Get Started, and sign in with a supported SSO identity provider.
details: A public-domain identity enters the Personal plan; a custom-owned domain starts a 14-day Enterprise trial.
primary_path.output: New tailnet account and plan assignment.
primary_path.success_signal: The Welcome to Tailscale page appears.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Plan assignment differs by identity domain.

primary_path.action: Select Business use or Personal use.
details: This is part of the onboarding sequence.
primary_path.output: Let's add your first device page.

primary_path.action: Select the first device's OS, download and install the client, then authenticate with the same identity.
primary_path.input: First supported device and SSO credentials.
primary_path.success_signal: The device appears in the browser onboarding window.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If device approval is enabled in an existing tailnet, an administrator approval state can appear; this new-tailnet quickstart does not introduce it.

primary_path.action: Select the second device's OS, copy the provided link to that device, install the client, and authenticate it.
details: The same tailnet identity is used.
primary_path.input: Second supported device and onboarding link.
primary_path.success_signal: Both devices display in the onboarding page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The second device must be accessible to complete this step.

primary_path.action: Select Take me home and review the admin console.
details: The admin console manages users, devices, DNS, permissions, and keys.
primary_path.success_signal: The Machines view can show enrolled devices with their Tailscale identities and addresses.

branches.condition: Signup begins from an installed client.
branches.path: Download and install the client first, then sign up and authenticate it to create the tailnet.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Account and installation order changes, not the two-device result.

branches.path: Use a tag identity and an authentication key as recommended by the add-device documentation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Admin permissions, key generation, and policy configuration replace interactive SSO.

friction_gates.description: A supported SSO identity is required and determines the initial plan route.
Business versus Personal use and device operating systems must be selected.
A native Tailscale client must be downloaded and installed on each device.
The documented terminal sequence requires access to a second device.

sources.title: Tailscale quickstart
evidence_supported: Complete two-device enrollment and console boundary

uncertainties.question: Does the under-10-minute claim include downloading and configuring both devices on every supported OS?
uncertainties.impact: Actual elapsed time varies with installers, app stores, permissions, and access to the second device.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quickstart video title provides the claim but does not define its timing envelope.
